sourceDesc.p: Derived from CDSL MW, PWG, PWK, AP90, and GRA source records for interoperability analysis.

projectDesc.p: tei-archival-profile-v0.1; TEI is used as the archival/textual model. Raw CDSL records are preserved as escaped source-entry quotes, while selected lexical structures are promoted into TEI entry, form, sense, citation, and relation elements.

revisionDesc.change: Included in the full machine-reviewed TEI profile.

sense.note: No stable machine definition could be extracted; source record is preserved in cit/quote.

listBibl.head: Machine-extracted citation index; source quotes preserve the archival text.

cit.quote: <L>50905.2<pc>285,3<k1>ku<k2>ku<e>1A ¦ as a separate word <s>ku</s> occurs only in the lengthened form <hom>3.</hom> <s>kU/</s>, <ab>q.v.</ab> <LEND>

<L>17404<pc>2-0303<k1>ku<k2>ku<h>1
1. {#ku#}¦
<div n="1"> 1) <ab>pron.</ab> <ab>interr.</ab>, erhalten in den <ab>advv.</ab> {#kutas, kutra, kuvid, kuha, kva#} und am Anfange von <ab>compp.</ab>, als Ausdruck {%des Mangelhaften, Schlechten%} <is>gaṇa</is> {#svarAdi#} zu <ls>P. 1,1,37.</ls>
<is>Vārtt</is>. 4 der <ls>SAUNĀGA</ls> zu <ls n="P.">2,2,18.</ls> <ls>AK. 3,4,32,2.</ls> (<ls>COLEBR. 3,4,28,2.</ls>) ({#pApakutsezadarTe#}).
<ls>H. an. 7,7</ls> und <ls>MED. <is>avy</is>. 11</ls> (ausser den eben angeführten <ab>Bedd.</ab> noch {#nivAraRe#}). Accent eines solchen <ab>comp.</ab>
<ls>P. 6,2,2</ls>, <is>Vārtt</is>. {#kuprAvfta#} {%schlecht bekleidet%}
<ls>R. 1,6,8.</ls> {#kutapasvin#} {%ein böser Büsser%}
<ls>PAÑCAT. 126,1.</ls> Ursprünglich hob {#ku#} nur {%das Ausserordentliche, Aussergewöhnliche%} einer Erscheinung hervor. Belege hiezu wird man im Folgenden finden. <ab>Vgl.</ab> 1. {#ka#},1. {#kava#},1. {#kA, kim#} und {#su#}, welches sich zum <ab>demonstr.</ab> {#sa#} verhält wie {#ku#} zu {#ka#} .
<div n="1">— 2) <lex>adv.</lex> {%wo%}: {#kU zWo^ devAvaSvinA\dyA di\vo ma^nAvasU#}
<ls>ṚV. 5,74,1.</ls> Mit {#cid#} {%wo immer, irgendwo%}: {#kU ci^tsa\tIrU\rve gA vi^veda#}
<ls n="ṚV.">9,87,8.</ls> <ab>Vgl.</ab> {#kva#} .
<LEND>

<L>28111<pc>2-068-c<k1>ku<k2>ku<h>3 <hom>3.</hom> {#ku#}¦ <lex>f.</lex> <div n="1">— 1〉 {%die Erde%} <ls>ĀRYABH. 2,1</ls>. {%Land, Erdboden%} <ls>Spr. 7789</ls>. <div n="1">— 2〉 {%die Basis eines Dreiecks%} (<ls>Comm. zu ĀRYABH. 2,8</ls>) oder {%einer anderen Figur%}. <div n="1">— 3〉 <ab>Bez.</ab> {%der Zahl eins%}. <LEND>

<L>10985<pc>0405-c<k1>ku<k2>ku {#ku#}¦ {%<ab>ind.</ab>%} A prefix implying ‘badness’, ‘deterioration,’ ‘depreciation’, ‘sin,’ ‘reproach,’ ‘littleness,’ ‘want,’ <lbinfo n="‘defici+ency"/> ‘deficiency’ &c. Its various substitutes are {#kad(kadaSva), kava(kavozRa), kA(kozRa)#}, {#kiM (kiMpraBuH)#}; <ab>cf.</ab> <ls>Pt. 5. 17.</ls> {@--Comp.@} {#--karman#} {%<ab>n.</ab>%} a bad deed, a mean act. {#--grahaH#} an unpropitious planet. {#--grAmaH#} a petty village or hamlet (without a king's officer, an {%agnihotrin%}, a physician, or a river). {#--cela#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} <lbinfo n="wear+ing"/> wearing bad or ragged garments. {#--caryA#} wickedness, evil conduct, <lbinfo n="improprie+ty"/> impropriety. {#--janman#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} low-born. {#--tanu#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} deformed, ugly. {#--(nuH)#} an epithet of Kubera. {#--taMtrI#} a bad lute. <lbinfo n="--ta#rkaH"/> {#--tarkaH#} {1} sophistical or fallacious <lbinfo n="argu+ment"/> argument. {2} a heterodox doctrine, <lbinfo n="free+thinking"/> freethinking; {#kutarkezvaByAsaH#} <lbinfo n="satataparapESu#nyamananam"/> {#satataparapESunyamananam#} <ls>G. L. 31.</ls> {#˚paTaH#} a <lbinfo n="sophisti+cal"/> sophistical mode of arguing. {#--tIrTaM#} a bad teacher. {#--dinaM#} an evil or <lbinfo n="unpropi+tious"/> unpropitious day. {#--dfzwiH#} {%<ab>f.</ab>%} {1} weak sight. {2} an evil eye, sinister eye (<ab>fig.</ab>). {3} an opinion or doctrine opposed to the Vedas, heterodox doctrines; [Page0406-a+ 56] <ls>Ms. 12. 95.</ls> {#--deSaH#} {1} a bad place or country. {2} a country where the necessaries of life are not available or which is subject to oppression. {#--deha#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} ugly, deformed. {#--(haH)#} an <lbinfo n="epi+thet"/> epithet of Kubera. {#--DI#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} {1} foolish, silly, stupid. {2} wicked. {#--nawaH#} {1} a bad actor {2} a sort of trumpet flower. {3} red arsenic. {#--nadikA#} a small river, rill; {#supUrA syAtkunadikA#} <lbinfo n="ls:Pt. 1.+ 25."/> <ls>Pt. 1. 25.</ls> {#--nATaH#} a bad master. {#--nAman#} {%<ab>m.</ab>%} a miser. {#--paTaH#} {1} a wrong road, bad way (<ab>fig.</ab> also). {2} a heterodox <lbinfo n="doc+trine"/> doctrine. {#--paTya#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} unwholesome, <lbinfo n="im+proper"/> improper. {#--parIkzaka#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} examining <lbinfo n="bad+ly"/> badly, not valuing rightly; <ls>Bh. 2. 15.</ls> {#--putraH#} a bad or wicked son. <lbinfo n="--pu#ruzaH"/> {#--puruzaH#} a low or wicked man. {#--pUya#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} low, vile, contemptible. {#--priya#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} disagreeable, contemptible, low, mean. {#--plavaH#} a bad boat; {#kuplavEH#} <lbinfo n="saMta#ran"/> {#saMtaran jalam#} <ls>Ms. 9. 161.</ls> {#--brahmaH, --brahman#} {%<ab>m.</ab>%} a bad or degraded Brāhmaṇa. {#--maMtraH#} {1} bad advice. {2} a charm used to secure success in a bad cause. {#--yogaH#} an inauspicious conjunction (of planets). {#--yogin#} {%<ab>m.</ab>%} a false devotee, impostor. {#--rasa#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} <lbinfo n="hav+ing"/> having bad juice or flavour. {#--(saH)#} a kind of spirituous liquor. {#--rUpa#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} ugly, deformed; <lbinfo n="ls:Pt.+ 5. 19."/> <ls>Pt. 5. 19.</ls> {#--rUpyaM#} tin. {#--vaMgaH#} lead. {#--vacas, --vAkya#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} abusive, bad, scurrilous; using abusive, or foul language. ({%--<ab>n.</ab>%}) abuse, bad language. {#--vajrakaM#} crystal; a stone resembling a diamond. {#--varzaH#} a sudden or <lbinfo n="vio+lent"/> violent shower. {#--vivAhaH#} a degraded or improper form of marriage; <lbinfo n="ls:Ms.+ 3. 63."/> <ls>Ms. 3. 63.</ls> {#--vfttiH#} {%<ab>f.</ab>%} bad behaviour. {#--vEdyaH#} a bad physician, quack. <lbinfo n="--SI#la"/> {#--SIla#} {%<ab>a.</ab>%} rude, wicked, unmannerly, <lbinfo n="ill+tempered"/> illtempered {#--zWalaM#} a bad place. {#--sarit#} {%<ab>f.</ab>%} a small river, rill; {#ucCidyaMte kriyAH#} {#sarvA grIzme kusarito yaTA#} <ls>Pt. 2. 85.</ls> {#--sftiH#} {%<ab>f.</ab>%} {1} evil conduct, <lbinfo n="wicked+ness"/> wickedness. {2} conjuring, magic. {3} <lbinfo n="ro+guery"/> roguery. {#--strI#} a bad woman. <LEND>

<L>2582<pc>0328<k1>ku<k2>ku/ {@kú,@}¦ fragender Deutestamm, aus kúa, kvā̀ gekürzt [<ab n="siehe">s.</ab> dort], enthalten in kútas, kútra, kuvíd, kúha. In Zusammensetzungen (wie ka-, ki-): sehr, gewaltig oder übel. <LEND>
